compiled_state.title: As an AI coding agent, I receive kudos for reducing nesting depth and eliminating duplicate code blocks detected via Python AST analysis
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement Python AST-based kudos triggers T7 and T13 — control-flow nesting depth REDUCTION and duplicate code block REMOVAL — using the algorithm and threshold confirmed by Spike S1.
**Why**: T7 and T13 recognize deep structural improvements that require AST-level analysis. They depend on Spike S1's findings to select the correct detection algorithm (AST hashing vs jscpd) and threshold.
**Scope**:
- IN: T7 and T13 trigger implementations; algorithm choice from S1 recommendation
- OUT: diff-to-sg-scope.sh (Story B); shell triggers (Story C); review triggers (Story D)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T7 awards kudos when REMOVED diff hunks contain control-flow nesting >= 4 levels deep (detected via ast.NodeVisitor with counter: visit_If/For/While/With/Try increment, generic_visit decrement) AND the resulting file's maximum nesting depth is lower than the original; T7 does NOT award kudos for merely having deeply nested code (must detect REDUCTION)
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T7
- When this story is complete, T13 uses the approach selected by Spike S1: if AST hashing (hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode())) — pure Python stdlib, threshold from S1; if jscpd — approved per CLAUDE.md rule 11 and listed as devDependency; awards kudos when >= 3 duplicate AST subtrees are removed in staged Python files
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T13
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
